note: Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/DoublyStochasticMatrix.lean:42 -- def doublyStochastic (R n) [Fintype n] ... : Submonoid (Matrix n n R), docstring 'a square matrix is doubly stochastic if all entries are nonnegative, and left or right multiplication by the vector of all 1s gives the vector of all 1s.' This IS the standard definition of a doubly stochastic matrix.
verify_note: decl exists in cache and mathlib4 checkout (line 42); source confirms nonneg + row/col sum = 1 via M *ᵥ 1 = 1 and 1 ᵥ* M = 1, matching the standard definition exactly; QID Q1243340 = 'doubly stochastic matrix' on Wikidata; exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Trigonometric.lean:78-79 -- 'The real cosine function, defined as the real part of the complex cosine' / nonrec def cos (x : ℝ) : ℝ := (cos x).re, inside `namespace Real`. This IS the cosine function.
verify_note: decl exists; source at lines 77-80 confirms docstring and definition verbatim as claimed, inside namespace Real so full name is Real.cos; this is unambiguously the standard cosine function, exact is correct.

note: Archive/Sensitivity.lean:409-410 -- theorem huang_degree_theorem (H : Set (Q m.succ)) (hH : Card H ≥ 2^m+1) : ∃ q, q ∈ H ∧ √(m+1) ≤ Card H ∩ q.adjacent. File docstring: 'A formalization of Hao Huang's sensitivity theorem: in the hypercube of dimension n≥1, if one colors more than half the vertices then at least one vertex has at least √n colored neighbors.' This IS Huang's sensitivity theorem (resolving the Sensitivity Conjecture).
verify_note: decl exists; file header (lines 16-20) and inline docstring 'Huang sensitivity theorem also known as the Huang degree theorem' (line 408) confirm this is a direct formalization of the named theorem; exact is correct.

note: Mathlib/Algebra/Module/ZLattice/Basic.lean:435 -- class IsZLattice (K) {E} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace K E] (L : Submodule ℤ E): a full-rank discrete ℤ-submodule of a real (or general normed) vector space E. The concrete integer lattice ℤ^n ⊂ ℝ^n is the motivating/standard example (span of the standard basis) but Mathlib has no decl named specifically for ℤ^n itself; ZLattice/ZSpan is the strict general framework it instantiates.
verify_note: decl exists at line 435, matches note; Wikidata Q1252145 confirms 'integer lattice' = ZZ^n specifically, and IsZLattice is a strictly more general class (any full-rank discrete Z-submodule of a normed space) that ZZ^n instantiates, not a decl named for ZZ^n itself -- 'generalization' with medium confidence is the honest label, not overclaimed as exact.
